flavorText: It digs holes on mountains, searching for food. It's so durable that being caught in a cave-in won't faze it.
It has a small heart and brain. Its stomach comprises most of its body, with enzymes to dissolve anything.
It meditates to heighten its inner energy and to float in the air. It eats one berry a day.
When attacked, it tackles its foe and covers them in cream. With its foe either blinded or astonished by deliciousness, Milcery flees.
It runs as fast as lightning strikes, shredding its opponents with its high-voltage claws.
Since it can't generate its own electricity, it sticks onto large-bodied Pokémon and absorbs static electricity.
Though its punches are powerful, this Pokémon's movements are sluggish. It blows icy bubbles from its mouth to immobilize its opponents.
When it feels threatened, it draws its limbs inside its shell and sprays water from its mouth.
While it prides itself on its varied kicking moves, it can also deliver powerful headbutts once its flames have heated up its forehead.
Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.
This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. Lileep once lived in warm seas that existed approximately 100,000,000 years ago.
When the stars flicker in the night sky, it flutters about, scattering a glowing powder.
While it is young, it uses the nutrients that are stored in the seed on its back in order to grow.
It can sprint at over 70 mph while carrying a human. The rider's body heat warms Cyclizar's back and lifts the Pokémon's spirit.
It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn't use it for anything other than foraging.
Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You'll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.
Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.
When it gets excited, it vents sparks from the gaps between its feathers. These unburnable feathers are used as a fireproof material.
Its territory has a radius of just over a mile. If any bird Pokémon should enter, it will show them no mercy.
Fletchling can be found in both rural and urban areas. They once had a huge territorial dispute with Squawkabilly. Fights broke out all over town.
The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.
These Pokémon can crush a car with no more than a headbutt. Bouffalant with more hair on their heads hold higher positions within the herd.
There are several appliances that Rotom can inspirit, but the first one to be developed was the electric fan.
It would claim another Pokémon's nest as its own if it finds a nest sufficiently comfortable.
It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate. Its fickleness is very popular among some.
Its extremely soft wings make no sound in flight. It silently sneaks up on prey without being detected.
Since it tells time precisely, some countries consider it to be a deity of wisdom, versed in the ways of the world.
It can evolve into a variety of forms. Eevee's genes are the key to solving the mysteries of Pokémon evolution.
A rowdy Pokémon with a lot of stamina. Once running, it won't stop until it hits something.
It's said to incinerate everything around it with lightning launched from its fur. Very little is known about this creature.
It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.
The steel section is Varoom's actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.
It digs holes on mountains, searching for food. It's so durable that being caught in a cave-in won't faze it.
Its metal body is durable but prone to retaining heat. It vents this heat from the slits in its tail.
At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.
It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.
It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.
From its spikes, it launches powerful blasts of electricity. Its red core contains an enormous amount of energy.
When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokémon's rotation speed increases sharply.
The two minigears that compose this Pokémon are closer than twins. They mesh well only with each other.
It is said to have the ability to grant any wish for just one week every thousand years.
Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.
It lives deep within the forest. Even after evolving into this splendid form, it hasn't given up on its petty misdeeds and pranks.
This Pokémon absorbs negative emotions and turns them into energy. It's popular with people who tend to think gloomy thoughts.
They live in groups, pestering and playing pranks on each other to polish their troublemaking skills.
This Pokémon is quick to anger, and it has no problem using its prodigious strength to get its way. It lives for duels against Obstagoon.
It can swallow a tire whole in one gulp. It secretes a horribly toxic fluid from the pores on its body.
It has a small heart and brain. Its stomach comprises most of its body, with enzymes to dissolve anything.
This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.
The leader, known as the brass, uses its extendible horn to issue orders to the others when it's time to change formation.
If it loses its pincers, they'll quickly regrow. The pincers are popular ingredients in paella since their shells produce a tasty soup stock.
A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.
It chooses a Pangoro as its master and then imitates its master's actions. This is how it learns to battle and hunt for prey.
Using the long fur on its arms like whips, it launches into combo attacks that, once started, no one can stop.
They seclude themselves in the mountains and devote themselves to training. The form of their kicks and chops differs from pack to pack.
It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions, such as joy and rage, in the form of waves.
It elegantly avoids attacks with dance-like steps, then launches a devastating blow in the same motion.
It meditates to heighten its inner energy and to float in the air. It eats one berry a day.
It never skips its daily yoga training. It heightens its inner strength through meditation.
A Rhyperior's brain is smaller than a Rhydon's, but the Rhyperior is stronger because it spends more energy on battling and less on brainpower.
It evolved to walk using only its hind legs, which has allowed it to expand its habitat even onto steep mountains.
Once it starts running, it won't stop—even if it crashes through boulders. This disposition is what's caused its habitat to expand.
This Pokémon overcame its sorrow to evolve a sturdy new body. Marowak faces its opponents bravely, using a bone as a weapon.
When the memory of its departed mother brings it to tears, its cries echo mournfully within the skull it wears on its head.
It was named after a mysterious object recorded in an old book. Its body seems to be metallic.
It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.
It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.
Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.
The yeast in Fidough's breath is useful for cooking, so this Pokémon has been protected by people since long ago.
Alcremie that have been cherished produce exquisite cream, so Trainers always raise them lovingly.
When attacked, it tackles its foe and covers them in cream. With its foe either blinded or astonished by deliciousness, Milcery flees.
It prefers areas with lots of flowers. A Comfey adorned with flowers is a happy Comfey, and the flowers gain healing effects.
The spirit of a person from a bygone age became this Pokémon. It rambles through ruins, searching for someone who knows its face.
They carry people and Pokémon, but the wind can catch them, so there can't be a fixed destination.
Because of the way it floats aimlessly, an old folktale calls it a “signpost for wandering spirits.”
A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.
It has incredible intellect and intuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.
It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.
The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.
It runs as fast as lightning strikes, shredding its opponents with its high-voltage claws.
With the long hairs on its back, this Pokémon takes in electricity from other electric Pokémon. It stores what it absorbs in an electric sac.
When carrying a Charjabug, Vikavolt can receive electricity from it and then rapidly fire powerful electromagnetic beams from its large jaws.
As it digests fallen leaves, it generates and stores electricity, which it can discharge from the tips of its jaws.
Since it can't generate its own electricity, it sticks onto large-bodied Pokémon and absorbs static electricity.
It blinds prey with an intense burst of light. With the prey incapacitated, the Pokémon swallows it in a single gulp.
It shoots positively and negatively charged electricity from the tips of its two antennae and stuns its opponents.
This Pokémon presses the tips of its tails onto an opponent and instantly sends over 20,000 volts of high-voltage electricity through them.
Research is progressing on storing lightning in Electabuzz so this energy can be used at any time.
Veluza has excellent regenerative capabilities. It sheds spare flesh from its body to boost its agility, then charges at its prey.
Drednaw lurks along the shoreline. When prey come to drink water, Drednaw stretches its neck out and chomps down on them.
A popular game among children is to lift up sticks that Chewtle have bitten onto to see whose Chewtle can hang on the longest.
Though its punches are powerful, this Pokémon's movements are sluggish. It blows icy bubbles from its mouth to immobilize its opponents.
It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.
It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.
Carracosta completely devours its prey—bones, shells, and all. Because of this, Carracosta's own shell grows thick and sturdy.
This Pokémon inhabited ancient seas. Although it can only crawl, it still comes up onto land in search of prey.
Lumineon swimming in the darkness of the deep sea look like stars shining in the night sky.
It absorbs sunlight with the pink areas of its skin, which then shine. This appears to be a form of camouflage.
Its long ears are superb sensors. It can distinguish the movements of living things on riverbeds.
The oil-filled end of its tail floats on water. It keeps Marill from drowning even in a strong current.
It loves crossing the sea with people and Pokémon on its back. It understands human speech.
Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.
While it prides itself on its varied kicking moves, it can also deliver powerful headbutts once its flames have heated up its forehead.
Fire energy gathers in the pads of its feet, raising their temperature. Once hot, Scorbunny's footpads can deal heavy damage to opponents.
Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.
When two Salazzle meet, they will use their pheromone gas to fight over the males in each other's group.
Only female Salandit can produce gas laden with pheromones. Males entranced by this gas will do whatever the females tell them.
This legendary Pokémon can scorch the world with fire. It helps those who want to build a world of truth.
This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.
It gallops at nearly 150 mph. With its mane blazing ferociously, it races as if it were an arrow.
Its legs grow strong while it chases after its parent. It runs in fields and mountains all day.
These Pokémon gather into groups and form colonies deep within forests. They absolutely hate it when strangers approach.
Toedscool lives in muggy forests. The flaps that fall from its body are chewy and very delicious.
When it decides to fight all out, it stands on its previously folded legs to enter Showdown Mode. It neutralizes its enemies in short order.
It has its third set of legs folded up. When it's in a tough spot, this Pokémon jumps over 30 feet using the strength of its legs.
Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.
The seeds attached to its cotton fluff are full of nutrients. It spreads them on the wind so that plants and other Pokémon can benefit from them.
It anchors itself in the ground with its single leg, then basks in the sun. After absorbing enough sunlight, its petals spread as it blooms brilliantly.
It spits sticky threads and winds them around branches, then swings nimbly from tree to tree in a pendulum-like motion.
This Rotom has possessed a lawn mower. It will mow down grass and flowers, then swagger around with pride at its accomplishments.
It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.
It has short legs and can't walk very fast, but its neck and tentacles can extend to over three times their usual length to nab distant prey.
This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. Lileep once lived in warm seas that existed approximately 100,000,000 years ago.
This Pokémon has the ability to move through time. Records describing it as a forest deity can be found from many different eras.
When the stars flicker in the night sky, it flutters about, scattering a glowing powder.
When the weather turns cold, lots of Ledyba gather from everywhere to cluster and keep each other warm.
